design_philosophy.core_loop: Draw lines to claim territory on playfield while avoiding the Qix and Sparx enemies; capture 75% of the screen to complete level
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward between slow careful drawing (double points) versus fast aggressive claiming; balancing progress toward goal against collision danger
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Territorial control and geometric mastery; solving spatial puzzles through drawing and area capture
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Unique abstract gameplay concept entirely unlike contemporary games; emphasizes spatial reasoning and speed-vs-safety decision making over reflexes or memorization; intentionally obtuse and 'mystifying' design that appeals to puzzle enthusiasts but alienates casual players

difficulty.lives_system: Player loses one life when Qix collides with marker during line drawing, when Sparx/Super Sparx hit marker, or when fuse timer reaches marker during stalled drawing
difficulty.checkpointing: Level-based progression; must complete each level by claiming required percentage before advancing
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency: player chooses between Slow Draw (safe, double points) and Fast Draw (risky, normal points); can strategically decide which areas to claim and in what order; difficulty escalates with additional Qixes starting level three and increasing Sparx/Super Sparx spawn rates

progression.upgrade_system: Scoring multiplier system: splitting/separating Qixes applies cumulative bonus multiplier (1x initially, increases by 1x per split, maximum 9x) applied to all subsequent level scores
progression.unlock_mechanism: Levels unlock sequentially upon reaching 75% territory capture threshold (adjustable 50-90%); additional complexity unlocks include dual Qixes at level three, additional Sparx waves, and Super Sparx mutations
replayability_hooks: High score chasing with scoring multiplier optimization
Speed-based scoring (Slow Draw vs Fast Draw strategy decisions)
Difficulty variation through adjustable capture percentage thresholds
Escalating Sparx complexity and Qix multiplicity

boss_design.philosophy: The Qix itself functions as a semi-boss obstacle rather than traditional boss; abstract geometric enemy with unpredictable graceful motion that doesn't actively hunt player but creates hazard through constant presence and collision detection during drawing phase
notable_bosses: Qix: primary obstacle entity with random unpredictable motion; collision during line-drawing causes life loss
Sparx: secondary enemies that traverse playfield edges; mutate into Super Sparx that can follow marker along uncompleted lines
Dual Qixes (Level 3+): two simultaneous Qix entities requiring territorial separation strategy for level completion

danger_signals: Qix collision during active line drawing
Sparx/Super Sparx intersection with marker position
Fuse timer detonation from stalled drawing
Spiral Death Trap (unescapable line drawing positions)
environmental_storytelling: Abstract geometric visual design with no narrative context
Color-coded capture methods (orange-red for Slow Draw, other colors for Fast Draw) communicate player strategy
Escalating visual complexity through additional Qixes and Sparx indicates progression difficulty increase
